session_authorization.scope: this session only; does NOT waive leakage discipline (5-null gate, formula_sha256 prereg, 5-fold screen before LOOCV all mandatory)

objective: Honest +ΔCCC over iter34 hybrid (LOOCV CCC=0.7366 on N=93) under FWER-adjusted gate, OR cleanly close the T1 ceiling story.

members.name: ordinal_cumulative_link_chain
members.screen_outcome: 5-fold screen failed promotion gate; LOOCV not run; F35-A wall data point

members.name: bayesian_2factor_LKJ_pooling
members.skip_reason: Tri-CLI consult (codex+gemini) converged on SKIP. Load-bearing architectural critique: per-subject latent z_s in R^2 vanishes for held-out LOOCV subjects (E[z_s|X_s]=0 from prior; inferring from item residuals leaks target). Adding encoder z_s=g(X_s) collapses to reduced-rank regression which RegressorChain already extracts. N=93 with K=500 cannot support joint Bayesian inference. Mechanism is structurally redundant with iter34, not orthogonal. Skipping pre-execution preserves FWER credibility budget.

members.name: phaselocked_item9_item12_slot_replacement

members.verdict: FAIL — composite Δ̄ vs iter34 = −0.0209, frac>0 vs iter34 = 0.013 (catastrophic); even vs iter12-honest-N=93 frac>0 = 0.907 < 0.95 loose gate. Per-item gains real (item 9 +0.42, item 12 +0.43) but do NOT aggregate to T1-sum gain because iter34 chain extracts equivalent or better cross-task signal. F35-C wall data point.

members.name: orthogonal_architecture_no_per_subject_latent

All viable candidates collapse: anatomical mixture-of-experts → stacked-meta blender (F58 ban); NUTS-without-per-subject-latent → isomorphic to iter34 + inner-CV-tuned K (F66/F67 wall); learned DAG chain → reparameterization of F70 RegressorChain (no orthogonal information). No viable 4th candidate orthogonal to slot A's loss-family axis, slot B's per-subject-latent axis, AND the F53/F56/F58 blending wall.
members.verdict: SKIPPED — F35-D 12th wall data point closes architecturally-orthogonal-without-per-subject-latent angle

members.name: post_stage2_site_additive_intercept
members.verdict: FAIL — Δ vs iter34 = −0.0105, paired-bootstrap frac>0 = 0.065. WPD signed-residual was real but NON-UNIFORM (concentrated in ~6 high-leverage subjects); flat per-site offset HURT low-severity WPD. Dominant residual is F61 regression-to-mean (Q1 over-prediction signed −1.034). F36-A 13th wall data point — closes site-additive-bias angle.

members.name: chainpool_phaselocked_injection
members.verdict: 5-fold screen FAIL — Δ̄ vs iter34 5-fold = +0.0076 across 3 seeds (per-seed: −0.006 / +0.018 / +0.011); paired-bootstrap vs iter34 frac>0 = 0.9252 (just below 0.95 nominal). Master P5 sanity (single seed=42): Δ_real_vs_v2 = −0.006 (probeD HURTS at seed=42); Δ_shuf_vs_v2 = +0.003 (shuf ≈ baseline, P5 PASS). Combined: the +0.008 mean is within seed-variance noise at N=92. Iter34's K=500 selector either ignores PL or picks noisy combinations. F36-D wall data point — chain-pool injection at this N produces no robust lift over iter34 V2-only baseline.

A.name: ordinal_cumulative_link_chain
A.hypothesis: Items 9-14 are ordinal (0-4). iter34's MSE Stage-2 throws away rank-distance information. A proper cumulative-link logit (ordinal) loss on each item residual, summed via the same 8-item chain × 3-base ensemble structure as iter34, recovers rank info that translates to >= +0.025 LOOCV ΔCCC vs iter34.
A.stage1: Ridge alpha=1.0 on H&Y + cv_yrs + cv_sex + cv_dbs (9 features, fold-local standardisation; same as iter34/iter5)

ordinal_bases_3: mord.LogisticAT (linear all-threshold ordinal regression, sklearn-compatible) — analog of ExtraTrees in iter34
LightGBM 4-binary-decomposition: train 4 LGB binary classifiers for P(item_i >= k), k in {1,2,3,4}; isotonic-monotone projection across k; E[item_i] = sum_k P(item_i >= k) — analog of LGB in iter34
NGBoost with k_categorical_logit ordered link (gradient-boosted ordinal probabilistic regression) — analog of XGB in iter34
stage2_architecture.ensemble_method: average of expected-score predictions across 3 bases per fold per seed
stage2_architecture.feature_selection: K=500 LGB-importance per fold (computed once on V2, shared across 3 bases)
stage2_architecture.cut_point_strategy: fold-local; pool across items 15+18 if item-specific cut-point unestimable (e.g., item 11 has sparse positive-class). Bayesian prior on cut-points: weakly informative N(0,1) centered at empirical fold quantiles.

A.kill_signature: if per-item Pearson residuals on items 11 (sparse positive class) and 13 (truncated 0-2 distribution) do NOT tighten vs iter34's per-item residuals, the ordinal lift didn't materialize at the items where it should matter most — slot dead.
A.wall_check_orthogonality: Axis 1 (different loss family) is mechanistically distinct from F65/F68/F70's chain-and-ensemble axes. F-iter17 'per-item ordinal + temperature' was POST-HOC affine recalibration, NOT in-chain cumulative-link likelihood. NGBoost was tried in T3 mission but as point regression, not as ordinal classification. mord has not been used in this codebase.
A.p_value_passing_strict_gate: estimated 0.25-0.40 a priori (rank-info lift competing with N=93 detectability noise)

B.name: bayesian_2factor_LKJ_pooling
B.hypothesis: The 6-item residual matrix is rank-2 (PC1 ~ 70% on items 9, 10, 12; PC2 ~ 12% on items 14 + 13). iter34's RegressorChain learns this empirically. An explicit 2-factor latent severity model with LKJ correlation prior on residuals + horseshoe sparsity on per-item feature loadings extracts the same structure with much stronger N=93 regularization.

stage2_architecture.model: numpyro factor analysis with 2 latent factors per subject, LKJ(eta=2) prior on residual covariance, horseshoe prior on per-item feature loadings, K=50 LGB-importance features per fold
stage2_architecture.inference: NUTS 1500 warmup + 1000 samples × 4 chains; SVI fallback if wall > 90 min/fold
stage2_architecture.gpu: RTX 4060 8 GB VRAM — caps batch via chain_method='vectorized' size
stage2_architecture.predict: posterior mean E[item | X] per subject

B.kill_signature: if posterior factor loadings do NOT separate items 9/10/12 (PC1) from 14/13 (PC2), the rank-2 prior wasn't doing the work — ordinary multi-task dressed in Bayesian.
B.wall_check_orthogonality: F58 falsified k=1 and k=2 META-STACK mixers (Ridge/OLS on top of OOF predictions). Slot B is a JOINT factor model, not a meta-stack — different inductive bias entirely. Not on dead list.
B.preregistration_script: run_t1_ceiling_push_slotB.py (DEFER until slot A complete; slow-think Sub-Phase 1 + tri-CLI Sub-Phase 2 mandatory before script write)

C.name: phaselocked_item9_item12_slot_replacement
C.hypothesis: Items 9 (chair-rise) and 12 (pull-test postural-stability) are TUG-phase-locked transients. iter34's K=500 LGB selector on V2 absorbs phase-segmented signal because the chain shares one feature pool across items. F50-style hypothesis-restricted item slots (small focused feature set per item) bypass K=500 absorption. Replacing iter34's per-item-9 + per-item-12 OOFs with these slot OOFs, summed for T1, gives +0.025 vs iter34.
C.stage1: for items 9 and 12: Ridge on H&Y + cv_yrs + cv_sex + cv_dbs (per-item, possibly hy_residual_item_v2 architecture)

stage2_architecture.feature_extraction: cache_phaselocked_item9.csv (TUG sit-to-stand impulse + thigh angular velocity peak) + cache_phaselocked_item12.csv (TUG turn / postural recovery, gated to TUG phase 5)
stage2_architecture.per_item_models: F50-style item_only and hy_residual_item_v2 variants screened, winner per item lockboxed
stage2_architecture.t1_composition: iter34 chain OOF for items {10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 18} + slot OOF for items {9, 12}; sum items 9-14 for T1

C.kill_signature: if items 9 OR 12 do NOT lift >= +0.04 LOOCV vs iter34's per-item OOF (extracted from lockbox_t1_iter34_hybrid_20260506_141720.json y_pred decomposition), the phase-locked features didn't help.
C.wall_check_orthogonality: F50 mechanism re-applied to items not yet attempted (iter17 covered 4, 6, 15, 16, 17, 18 — items 9 and 12 untouched in hypothesis-restricted slot architecture). Phase-locked features for item 9 in iter4 TUG-microscope were GLOBAL (all items shared), not per-item-slot.
